content: The article discusses a recent Silicon Valley visit by a Chinese investor who reviewed the AI industry’s extreme competition. It describes how firms like Meta are using competitors’ tools such as Anthropic’s Claude, abandoning code‑security norms, and how engineers spend nearly their entire salary on token usage. One engineer remarks that even Silicon Valley cannot keep up with itself, highlighting the relentless, unsustainable arms race dominating the AI sector.

title: 预算 400 左右，有没有支持 TF 卡+6GB 内存以上的手机推荐？主要跑 Tyrano 和 Chill with you，支持 TF 卡的游戏手机
content: Looking for a phone around 400 CNY that supports a TF (microSD) card slot and at least 6 GB of RAM. The device will primarily run Tyrano and Galgame titles, and the game “Chill With You” (a tomato clock app). Desirable features include an AMOLED display for good visual quality, ability to unlock and flash official firmware (BL), and a clean Android experience with quick access to Gemini. The phone can be new or used. Suggestions should meet these specs.

title: 当敲代码如呼吸一样简单，程序员还剩下什么？
content: After open‑sourcing a project, the author gained about 400 stars in two weeks, largely due to steady product work rather than promotion. He notes that AI now writes correct code as long as requirements are clear, so coding itself is no longer a bottleneck. The real competitive edge for programmers will be imagination—creating novel ideas—and promotion—getting those ideas adopted. He urges developers to build self‑media presence to secure future opportunities.

title: Welcome to Maintainer Month: Celebrating the people behind the code
content: Maintainer Month celebrates open‑source maintainers by offering new tools, resources, and community events. Projects now get granular contribution limits, PR archiving, and access to guides on accessibility and workflow control. Partner organizations such as Sentry, OpenJS Foundation, and Daytona provide tools, compute credits, and conference tickets. Over 20 events—conferences, hackathons, and discussions—focus on security, licensing, and corporate support. A vetted Maintainer Community and partner packs extend support, while financial sponsorship and public recognition help sustain maintainers’ vital work.
source: The GitHub Blog - Abigail Cabunoc Mayes

The table stores AI competitions, hackathons, and training camp links, with fields for title, URL, status, start and end dates. The bot helps participants avoid missing deadlines and can be updated continuously.

title: LearningCircuit/local-deep-research

title: Should I Run Plain Docker Compose in Production in 2026?
content: The article discusses whether plain Docker Compose remains suitable for production use in 2026. It examines Compose’s simplicity and limitations, compares it to container orchestration solutions like Kubernetes, and highlights factors such as scalability, multi‑host deployment, networking, and automation. The piece encourages readers to evaluate current project needs, consider modern tooling, and make an informed choice between keeping Docker Compose or adopting more robust orchestrators for production workloads.

title: Building Blackstone, Backing Costco, with Tony James
content: Tony James discusses building long‑lasting firms like Blackstone and backing companies such as Costco. He traces his career from early days at DLJ to shaping Blackstone’s growth, emphasizing the importance of structure, culture, incentives, and talent development. James highlights how early identification of structural opportunities in leveraged buyouts and private markets created lasting competitive advantages. He also covers leadership, succession, and the need for long‑term thinking to sustain performance.

title: 境外 DNS 服务商 ECS (EDNS Client Subnet) 功能效果完整对比报告(2026 年 05 月） ，供大家参考使用
content: This report compares the ECS (EDNS Client Subnet) routing effectiveness of six DNS providers—AdGuard (unfiltered), Google, AdGuard (filtered), Quad9, OpenDNS, and NextDNS—using DoH queries from Japanese, Chinese, US, and Brazilian /24 subnets for Amazon, GitHub, Netflix, YouTube, Twitch, and Apple. AdGuard unfiltered scores highest (25/30), accurately routing all domains. Google and filtered AdGuard follow closely; Quad9, OpenDNS, and NextDNS perform poorly, especially for GitHub and Netflix. The report also recommends Singapore-based proxies for lowest latency and best ECS determinism.

title: 用中转站省钱，怎么知道没踩坑？
content: The article warns that many “intermediate nodes” offering cheaper AI model access can manipulate requests, swap models, truncate context, inject malicious code, or misrepresent usage. A study found some free and paid routes injecting code and even stealing crypto wallets. The authors created a Probe Kit that tests for model tampering, request alteration, accurate billing, and safe tool calls by making real API calls (≈$0.5). title: 大型挂机现场：马斯克的55万英伟达GPU，利用率才11%
content: Elon Musk’s xAI operates about 550,000 NVIDIA GPUs but only uses roughly 11% of them. The low utilization stems from coordination challenges, memory bandwidth limits, network latency, and intermittent training workloads. Larger clusters suffer idle time that worsens efficiency. While other firms like Meta and Google reach 43–46% usage, xAI is working to raise its rate to 50% through infrastructure and software stack improvements. The case highlights that buying GPUs is just the first step; effective usage is the true challenge in AI.

title: 抖音小游戏&微信小游戏的难度和收益如何？
content: Creating simple mini‑games for Douyin (TikTok) or WeChat can be moderately difficult if you have no prior game‑development experience. You’ll need to learn a platform SDK (Lua for WeChat, JavaScript or Unity for Douyin), design basic graphics, and adhere to each platform’s content and monetization rules. The publishing process involves preparing Chinese content, passing regulatory checks, and following submission guidelines, which is not overly complex but requires some effort. After approval, earnings come from in‑app purchases, ads, or blue‑coin revenue; revenue varies with user engagement and traffic but can provide modest, steady income. A personal account usually suffices; no additional server purchase is needed for simple games, though more complex features might require hosting. Douyin offers built‑in effects that can ease development.

content: ByteDance’s AI service “Doubao” is introducing paid subscriptions at mid‑2026. The plans range from 68 ¥/month for a standard tier to 5 088 ¥/year for a professional tier, aimed at complex, high‑compute tasks such as PPT generation, data analysis, and video creation. The move reflects the company’s need to offset growing compute costs as daily token usage has surged from 0.12 trillion to 120 trillion. Doubao’s free version will remain for everyday use, while paid tiers target users who need stable, high‑performance AI.

title: 被AI抢走工作的人，在忙什么？
content: Workers displaced by AI face shrinking roles, higher workloads, and wage cuts. Investment analysts, programmers, designers, and translators report gradual automation of routine tasks, leading to layoffs or job re‑specification that demands new skills. Many pivot to learning AI tools, agent development, or broader business knowledge to stay relevant. Competition intensifies as firms broaden hiring to include AI competency, requiring candidates to blend technical proficiency with an understanding of human value and market needs.

title: 过去10年美股涨幅TOP 10：从NVDA的239倍到Celsius的73倍，每只股票背后都对应一个时代
content: Over the last decade (2016‑2026) the U.S. market’s 10 largest stock‑growers totaled more than 2,000 % cumulative gains, with NVIDIA alone climbing 239‑fold. The top performers—NVIDIA, Celsius, Super Micro, Axon Enterprise, Tesla, Builders FirstSource, Shopify, Enphase Energy, Palo Alto Networks, and AMD—shared common traits: they rode major technology cycles, shifted toward SaaS or platform models, endured 2‑3 major drawdowns, maintained stable leadership, and were largely unseen before their ascent. These ten‑fold returns are exceptionally rare; most investors should prefer diversified, long‑term index exposure.

title: 五一最贵旅游胜地，批量「诈骗」全球中产？
content: In 2026, Nepali authorities raised the Everest South‑Slope permit fee to $15,000, making the climb a high‑cost adventure. Yet a sophisticated fraud scheme using excess sodium bicarbonate—delivered in “hot soup” by Sherpa guides—induces staged high‑altitude sickness. This triggers expensive helicopter rescues and insurance payouts, while insurers, helicopters, and hospitals collude to inflate claims. The scheme, which cost roughly $20 million and halted nearly 4,800 climbers, demonstrates how trust and remote conditions enable elaborate insurance scams.

title: 打造全球超大型数据中心，到底有多难？
content: Building a global mega data center, like Meta’s 5‑GW Hyperion project, demands unprecedented engineering feats. Foundations must handle ultra‑heavy GPU cabinets (over 1.5 t each) and high heat loads. Massive power (up to 5 GW) requires custom on‑site plants or close grid ties, often pushing local electricity prices up. Cooling shifts from air to costly liquid systems, while network bandwidth must support peak GPU traffic. These hurdles elevate construction costs, timelines, and environmental impact, redefining industry standards.

content: The article reexamines the long‑held view that Neanderthals were intellectually inferior to modern humans. It argues that this stereotype lacks solid evidence and highlights recent studies showing significant brain differences even among contemporary populations, such as between Chinese and Americans—differences that surpass those between Homo sapiens and Neanderthals. The piece calls into question the simplistic narrative that Neanderthals were evolutionary failures due to cognitive deficiency.

title: Anthropic 接近与黑石、高盛等达成 15 亿美元的合资协议
content: Anthropic is negotiating a $1.5 billion joint venture with Black Rock, Goldman Sachs, and other Wall Street firms. Each partner, including Anthropic and Hellman Friedman, is expected to contribute roughly $300 million. The venture aims to fund private‑equity‑backed companies and expand Anthropic’s AI capabilities. The partnership reflects the growing interest of financial institutions in AI-driven investment opportunities.

title: 英伟达黄仁勋痛批 Anthropic CEO，呼吁 AI 行业领袖慎言慎行、以事实为据
content: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang criticized Anthropic CEO Daniel Amodei in a podcast, arguing that AI leaders should speak cautiously and base claims on facts. Huang disputed Amodei’s assertion that AI could replace 50 % of entry-level white‑collar jobs in the next few years and dismissed dramatic warnings that AI could destroy humanity, calling them irresponsible. He urged the industry to avoid “god‑like” rhetoric and to focus on realistic, evidence‑based discussions about AI’s economic and societal impact.

title: 红果回应：VIP 付费非新增，仅限极少量影视，短剧仍免费
content: Red Guo’s VIP subscription is not a new feature; only a small number of copyrighted titles require VIP access, as dictated by licensors. All short dramas remain free, and the VIP membership—which also removes ads—has existed since launch. The platform emphasizes that its core free‑to‑watch model is unchanged and debunks rumors that short dramas now cost money.

title: 马斯克寻求就 OpenAI 诉讼达成和解
content: Elon Musk contacted OpenAI co‑founder Greg Brockman two days before a court hearing to discuss a settlement in his lawsuit against OpenAI and Microsoft. He is seeking leadership changes and a $150 billion damages award, arguing that OpenAI stole his ideas. The lawsuit and its demands have attracted media coverage and criticism from OpenAI, which accuses Musk of obstructing AGI development.
